body_txt: Like the Badass Longcoat in modern settings, a character who wears a long robe in a fantasy setting will invariably be Badass. Like the Badass Cape , this can be used to dramatic effect by billowing in the wind , but it also has the advantage of being potentially All Encompassing to add an air of mystery. Plus, it looks good with a hood . Expect many monks to be this. Dramatic Wind aside, in a Fight Scene , a battle can be made so much easier if your movements are being concealed (foot-work, on-coming punches, etc.). Also, there's a sadistic joy to be taken from unnerving an enemy who can't see the damage he does nor the point he's aiming at. Suffice it to say, a person in a robe is an enigma both in battle and outside of it. As per Rule of Cool , this can be used to produce many impressive visual effects: anytime this character jumps (usually from a great height), they'll appear to have wings ; when the character spins, they'll take on the look of a violently twisting spiral; when they dash or rush forward, they look like a human bullet. If martial arts are involved, odds are good that the robe will have Dragons Up the Yin Yang . Compare with Robe and Wizard Hat which is often more oriented to magic-users and Black Cloak which is generally worn by evil characters.
